description:
Trigger: cargo test runs unit tests in parallel threads in a single process. The tests at secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:621, 684, 694 call std::env::remove_var("PKCS11_LIB") to ensure the env var is unset for that test. The integration tests at lines 705-776 read PKCS11_LIB. The umbrella crate tests across the workspace may also touch PKCS11_LIB.

Bad behavior: env vars are process-global. While test A is mid-from_uri reading the env var, test B's remove_var can clear it, causing test A to spuriously fail. This is a real source of CI flakes that may not reproduce locally if tests happen to be ordered differently.

In Rust 2024 edition, std::env::set_var and std::env::remove_var become unsafe fn precisely because of this hazard. The workspace is on edition 2021 today (Cargo.toml line: edition = "2021"), so it compiles, but the bug is the same — the new edition just forces you to acknowledge it.

Fix: do not mutate the process env in tests. Refactor from_uri to accept an optional library path / pin via a builder, then test the builder API directly. Or use a process-global Mutex to serialize tests that mutate env (e.g. once_cell::sync::Lazy<Mutex<()>>) and lock it in every env-touching test. Or run those tests with #[test] under a #[serial] attribute (serial_test crate).
title: Tests use std::env::remove_var/set_var which races with parallel tests in same process
